tools.name: create_wedding_project
tools.description: Create a new anonymous Wedding Seat project (no login). Returns a projectKey (your secret handle for all later calls) plus the workspace URL the couple opens in a browser. The projectKey is the ONLY way to access the project again — keep it.

tools.name: add_guests
tools.description: Add guests to a project (deduped by name+email). Provide the projectKey from create_wedding_project and a list of guests.

tools.name: generate_seating
tools.description: Auto-generate a reception seating plan: creates round tables and seats every guest with the constraint-aware solver (keeps must-sit-together groups together). Returns how many were seated and a link to the visual editor.

tools.name: get_project_summary
tools.description: Read the current state of a project: guest counts by RSVP status, seating tables/capacity, and budget totals. Read-only.

tools.name: list_culture_packs
tools.description: List the available wedding culture packs (key, English name, cluster, sides). Use a pack key as the `culture` argument to create_wedding_project. Grounding/read-only.

tools.name: list_tools
tools.description: List Wedding Seat's free planning tools (seating chart, guest list, budget, checklist, invitation wording) with their public URLs. Grounding/read-only.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "create_wedding_project" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create a new anonymous Wedding Seat project (no login). Returns a projectKey (your secret handle for all later calls) plus the workspace URL the couple opens in…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: add_guests, list_tools · Private data: create_wedding_project · Public sink: add_guests.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: add_guests, list_tools · Private data: create_wedding_project · Public sink: add_guests

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
